untranslatable_reason: This signal is a behavioral/tactical trading rule about actively soliciting latent demand from counterparties rather than a price-action or technical indicator-based signal. The entry conditions reference: (1) 'Order submission cost exceeds 5 basis points' — not a market indicator available in price/volume/technical data; (2) 'Counterparty has executed related trades in past 12 months' — requires counterparty-level transaction history not in technical indicators; (3) 'Current bid-ask spread exceeds 25 basis points' — bid-ask spread data not available in allowed indicators; (4) 'Block size is >500 shares or >$250k notional' — requires order flow or position sizing data. The exit conditions reference counterparty responses ('Counterparty declines once') which are behavioral signals, not market data. This is a microstructure/liquidity provision strategy that cannot be expressed using only OHLCV-based technical indicators.
translation_notes: Signal MICROSTRUCTURE_DRY_15_1 describes a block trading solicitation tactic requiring real-time bid-ask spreads, transaction costs, and counterparty interaction tracking. These operational variables fall outside the scope of technical indicators. To implement this signal would require a custom module with access to order book data, historical trade counterparty records, and explicit cost/spread feeds.
